title: Field pit-wave and strategy momentum
summary: F1 strategy is coupled: when several cars pit, nearby teams react to cover, undercut, or extend.
rationale: F1 strategy is coupled: when several cars pit, nearby teams react to cover, undercut, or extend. Current best includes field medians and immediate neighbor differences, but not same-lap or recent-lap pit-wave signals from the whole race field.
implementation_hint: For each Race+LapNumber, compute count/proportion of PitStop=1, median TyreLife of pitted cars, min/mean TyreLife among non-pitters, and compound-specific pit rates. Add lagged field pit counts for previous 1-3 laps by Race, plus cumulative stops so far per Race and per Compound. Exclude the row’s own PitStop from same-lap aggregate when possible to avoid self-dominance.
expected_effect: Can identify strategic pit windows and reaction laps that tyre-life-only features miss, especially when many drivers pit around the same lap.
risk: Same-lap field aggregates may be unrealistic if the intended prediction setting is strictly online per driver before observing other cars’ current-lap pit status. Compare with a lag-only variant under grouped CV.
